target.name: Material
target.founder: Gabe Elias, Christopher Reyes, Miles Dotson

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Material is a Seed-stage climate and energy technology company founded by Gabe Elias, Christopher Reyes, and Miles Dotson that has raised $7.1M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private frontier-tech companies in the Climate & Energy sector, including peers such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape, supplemented by one public comparable (LNZA).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis, using total capital raised as a baseline proxy of $7M and applying a typical 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $18M. Peer-Median analysis examined three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies—Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M—yielding a median comparable valuation of $150M with a range of $110M to $200M.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a valuation range of $15M (low) to $172M (high), with a median implied value of $84M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty regarding which methodological anchor better reflects Material's position: the stage-progression approach anchors to capital deployed, while peer medians reflect market pricing of later-stage frontier-energy companies. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of Series A peer multiples for a Seed-stage company and whether Material's technology differentiation justifies positioning toward the upper or lower end of the derived range.
